things.name: Gold Museum (Museo del Oro)
things.description: The world's finest pre-Columbian gold collection — 55,000 pieces including the El Dorado ceremonial raft

things.name: Monserrate Sanctuary
things.description: 3,152-metre hilltop shrine above Bogotá — cable car or funicular with panoramic city views

things.name: La Candelaria Historic Centre
things.description: Colonial quarter of cobbled streets, street art, emerald sellers and the historic Primatial Cathedral

things.name: Ciclovía Sunday
things.description: Every Sunday 121 km of roads close to cars — millions of Bogotans cycle, skate and jog through the city

things.name: Coffee Region (Eje Cafetero)
things.description: UNESCO World Heritage coffee landscape — jeep tours through the wax palm valleys and coffee farm visits

things.name: Botero Museum
things.description: Free museum of Fernando Botero's famously rounded figures — also houses his impressive Impressionist collection

things.name: Usaquén Flea Market
things.description: Sunday artisan market in the cobblestone colonial neighbourhood of Usaquén — antiques, food and live music

things.name: Salt Cathedral of Zipaquirá
things.description: Underground cathedral carved inside an active salt mine — one of the most spectacular churches in the world

things.name: Andrés Carne de Res
things.description: Colombia's most famous restaurant and nightclub — a mind-blowing sensory experience in the town of Chía

things.name: Paloquemao Flower Market
things.description: Daily wholesale flower market — Colombia is the world's second largest flower exporter and the colours are extraordinary
